title: cardamine pratensis subsp. typica
intro: Cardamine pratensis subsp. typica, also known as the common lady's mantle or marsh cress, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Cardamine pratensis subsp. typica include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
